key_points: Magnetic field lines represent the direction and strength of a magnetic field around a conductor
The strength of magnetic field around a conductor depends on the current and distance from the conductor
A solenoid produces a strong and nearly uniform magnetic field inside it
Electromagnetic induction occurs when there is a change in magnetic field around a conductor
Electric generators convert mechanical energy into electrical energy based on electromagnetic induction
examples: Crane magnets lifting heavy iron scrap using electromagnets
Electric motor used in washing machines and grinders
Hydroelectric power plants using generators to produce electricity
Fuse protecting appliances by breaking the circuit during excessive current
formulas: Fleming's Left-Hand Rule → Determines direction of motion of a conductor
Fleming's Right-Hand Rule → Determines direction of induced current
Power transmission principle → High voltage reduces energy loss
exam_tips: Understand both Fleming's left-hand and right-hand rules
Practice explaining the working of electric motor and generator with diagrams
Learn safety measures like earthing, fuse and circuit breakers
Focus on reasoning questions related to electromagnetic induction
